title: PayPal's New $44 Billion Pay Later Partner: Is the Stock a Buy Now?
text: PayPal has been an early leader in buy now, pay later credit, a profitable but higher-risk area of lending. With KKR now set to buy essentially all of its European BNPL loans, PayPal is de-risking its balance sheet and freeing up a lot of cash to deploy in other ways.

title: PayPal to sell up to $44B of its buy now, pay later loans in Europe to KKR
text: PayPal Holdings Inc (NASDAQ:PYPL) said it has reached a multi-year agreement to sell up to €40 billion ($4.37 billion) of its European buy now, pay later (BNPL) loan receivables to private equity firm KKR. Under the deal, the payments company said KKR's private credit funds and accounts will acquire substantially all the European BNPL loan portfolio held on PayPal's balance sheet at the close of the transaction and will also acquire future originations of eligible BNPL loans.

title: PayPal Selling $43 Billion in EU BNPL Loans to KKR
text: PayPal is selling more than $43 billion worth of its European buy now, pay later (BNPL) loans. The payments company announced Tuesday (June 20) it was selling loans originated in France, Spain, Germany, the U.K. and Italy to investment firm KKR.

title: Options Bulls Blast PayPal Stock on BNPL Purchase

title: PayPal Expects Additional $1 Billion in Stock BuyBacks Following BNPL Deal
text: KKR-managed funds and accounts will will purchase up to €40 billion of PayPal's buy now, pay later loan receivables originated in Europe.

title: PayPal boosts buyback expectations as it announces new deal with KKR
text: PayPal Holding Inc. PYPL, +0.88% has struck an arrangement with KKR through which KKR-managed funds and accounts will buy up to €40 billion of PayPal's buy-now-pay-later receivables originated in several European countries, the companies said Tuesday. The deal is expected to close in the second half of the year and initially generate about $1.8 billion in net proceeds that PayPal will use to return cash to shareholders and for general corporate purposes.

title: PayPal and KKR Announce Exclusive Multi-Year Relationship for European Pay Later Receivables
text: KKR to purchase up to €40 billion of eligible current and future PayPal Pay Later loans originated in Europe PayPal expects to allocate approximately $1 billion to incremental share repurchases this year; updated outlook from approximately $4 billion to approximately $5 billion in total share repurchases in 2023 SAN JOSE, Calif. and NEW YORK , June 20, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- PayPal Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: PYPL) and KKR, a leading global investment firm, today announced the signing of an exclusive multi-year agreement for a €3 billion replenishing loan commitment under which private credit funds and accounts managed by KKR will purchase up to €40 billion of buy now, pay later (BNPL) loan receivables originated by PayPal in France, Germany, Italy, Spain, and the United Kingdom.
